Here's the topic of the day. At the end of yesterday's live training, the person who put the training on, like, put me on the spot. And he said to me, Siri and Bailo, you're about to step in a hole. So be careful. There he goes. All right. I've got a guy following us. It's high value today, guys. You got to end up my iPhone today, right? Anyway, so I got to be careful with Siri's about to kill himself, right? But they had a mock door on the stage, right? And they wanted me to show us what your pitch was going door to door. Because, guys, I broke all the records in the door to door industry. I've sold mortgages door to door. I've sold meat and seafood door to door stereo systems. You name it, I've sold everything, right? And the irony is that there are no magic words when it comes to those first few seconds, whether it's in person or over the phone, right? I've always told you that those first few seconds, it's about being perceived sharp, enthusiastic, expert in your field, right? So when I gave them my pitch, I used the mortgage industry as an example, right? So let me just tell you this great story. So when I was in 2001, rates had just dropped, right? So everybody in their grandmother was refinancing their home. And it was right before everyone in their grandmother tried to become a mortgage broker, right? So I had this idea. I'm going to go out there and just simply go business to business to business to business. Every owner is there. He owns a home. I go to small businesses, right? And I'll just walk in and say, hey, what rate you're paying, right? And I'll refinance the house, right? So at the time, I was a good friend of mine back then, owned a small mom and pop mortgage company. And he had asked me to write him a telemarketing script. So I did that as a favor. And I tested the strip myself. And the first call I made, I closed $8,000 property. I'm like, damn. This is something here, right? I said, I'm going to actually try to grow this. That's why I got into the mortgage business, right? Then I came with a door-to-door idea, because no one was dealing it, right? So I said to my partner's wife, hey, Shelly, is it one of you come with me? No. And I'll take you and I'll show you how to do this. Very simple. You walk down one side of the street. I'll walk down the other. You put a little clipboard in your hand and just walk in and say, hey, you're a mortgage broker. What rate you're paying? Ask for the owner and so forth. She's like, oh, great, great. I love to sell, right? So we drove the car in Long Island at the time, right? We drove to, it was New Rochelle. So it was one of these broad streets, two-way traffic, right? Stores lined up, mom and pop shops down this way, mom and pop shops down that way, right? So I said, Shelly, you take the right side of the street. I'll take the left side of the street, right? So I get out of my car, walk into the first place. It's a restaurant. Owner's not there. Then no one spoke English. No good, right? Next place I go, it was called Dorosa Seafood. Phil Dorosa. I still remember his name. Walk in, second door, bam. Made $8,000. I'm like, oh my mother. I'm like, I am on to something here, right? I'm like, two doors later, bam. Another sale, $6,000. Three doors later, bam. Another sale, $12,000. I had made $20,000 in two hours, all right? And I was like, the whole time, I'm saying to myself, oh man, Shelly must be smashing it. I can't wait to be like two people just jumping for joy. And just so you know, guys, this happened right after I got indicted. I was broke. So I just kind of cracked the code, right? And I'm like, man, I'm gonna do this all over again, right? So I crossed the street like a victorious warrior, $20,000 richer. I'm looking for Shelly. Can't find her. And all of a sudden I see a little bench. I see this like figure hunched over and like, she's crying hysterically on the bench. I'm like, Shelly, what happened? It's impossible. Nobody wants to do this. It's you can't. I'm like, Shelly, I just made $20,000. She's like, what? I'm like, Shelly, I'm like, what? I'm like, I don't understand. I'm like, follow me. I grabbed her, all right? I said, just stay up straight and just walk behind me. I went down the same row of stores that she adjusts. I said, you hit every store, she was like, hit every single store. Walk down the same row of stores, okay? And close three people. The second door I hit was a jeweler, $5,000. The last one was an auto body, a big one, $15,000. I think it was the better dead-o water, Phil's the better dead-o, I think was his name, right? So I'm not making this, this is a true story, right? And like that they made like $40,000, $50,000 that first day doing it, all right? And Shelly was like, I don't understand. I went down, what she was doing? She was walking in like, hi, I'm, I'm my name Shelly. I'm from like mom and pop, no name mortgage company. And I would love to be your, you know, I'm like, hey, my name's Jordan Memorial, what's your, all I did. I'm like, hey, who's the owner, is the owner here? That was it, yeah, I'm there. Hey, got a mortgage broker in there, at what rate are you currently playing in the house? That's how fast, like just busy. These people are busy, they're working. Hey, my name's Jordan Memorial, I'm a mortgage broker, at what rate are you currently playing? There's eight and a half, I'm like, oh, I could save you a ton of money. I took that and that was the, oh, I could save you a ton of money. I took out my notebook, okay, so whatever you're living. And I start asking, I do my straight line, non-invasive questions, that, and I take my calculator, boom, before I know, guys, what was the difference between Shelley and I? I'll tell you what it was. I walked in with confident body language, looked the person right in the eye, I said, hey, who's the owner around? Like, excuse me, may I please speak to the business owner here, I'm like, hey, who's the owner? I'm the owner, already, I'm, hey, my name's Jordan, I'm a mortgage broker in the area, what rate you currently paying on your mortgage? That's it! No magic words. And this is what I had explained to the door-to-door place, but guys, it's not about what you say, it's how you look, it's how you make odd contact, it's the authority you bring, how you dress. Also, she was dressed like sort of like she's a pretty girl, but she was not dressed like with authority. I walked out of sport jacket on, all right, I had a nice little shirt, right? People see you, they size you up in a split second, on the phone you got four seconds, but if you think that, like, this is not really, I'm sort of exaggerating, I am not, guys, it all happens in those first few seconds. Now, you don't get to close the sale because of those first few seconds, but you stop yourself from blowing the sale, or you open up the possibility to influence. She walked in and people, they just, they're like, I'm not interested, it's not expert, and you get it, I walk in, they look at me, there's this energy, it's an energy flow, I want you to focus on this. Whether you're in person or on the phone today and all week long, remember you exude an energy force. It comes out of you in the form of your body language and your tonality, how you make odd contact, how you shake hands, the first few words you say, reason for the cold, is this urgency in your tone? Don't ever forget that, because what happens, even myself, by the way, if I'm doing something long enough that becomes so systematic and pattern-like that you forget that it's the color, it's the vivaciousness that actually brings it to life. You can't allow this to get ordered down one or two magnitudes with enthusiasm and poignancy, just cause you do it all the time. Every pitch is your first pitch this week. Trust me, every pitch is your first one, go in there, hey, my name's Bright Smile. Hey, my name's, hi, is the only one around? Not like, excuse me, can I please speak? That's not what they want. They want someone who's authority, expert, sharp on the ball, it's gonna solve their problems, help them achieve their goals, and someone who's also polite, non-threatening, and is just like them. All the things about the straight line, they all wrap up into one, but it happens, it starts in those first couple of seconds. Now, if you blow the first three or four seconds, yeah, you got 10 more seconds of cat jump ball, if you're really, after that, guys, you're done, you can't influence anyone, all right? So this is the week where we're really gonna focus on just being right at those first two seconds, sharp on the ball, face to face sales, that first quarter of a second, that first impression is everything. I want you to put yourself, look at it this way, this is the week where you're gonna put yourself in the perfect pole position to close every sale, starting from five seconds forward. Make sure those first five seconds open you up for the possibility, focus on that, and the rest will get much easier, and we'll talk again tomorrow, see you then.
